_comment: The BUNDLED STARTER CHANNEL for Mainguard's dynamic agent CLIs (P2-22 §J-5).
This ships inside Mainguard.Agents as an embedded resource, so CLI selection works out of the box with
no hosted Mainguard channel: the user picks CLIs during OOBE (or later in settings), and each is
fetched from its pinned npm tarball URL, sha256-verified against this file, then installed INSIDE
the VM at /home/mainguard/mainguard/adapters (npm --prefix) and bind-mounted READ-ONLY into every agent
sandbox at /opt/mainguard/adapters. Nothing is baked into the agent image — installing a CLI later
reaches every new sandbox with no image rebuild.
PINS ARE LOAD-BEARING — but since the managed updater (AgentCliUpdateService) they are the OFFLINE
FALLBACK, not a ceiling: an install first resolves the npm registry's CURRENT release, sha256-pins
that exact tarball as a local override, and installs it through the same verify path (EnsureLatestAsync).
These bundled pins govern only when the registry is unreachable — and they are still concrete
(AdapterManifest.Parse REFUSES @latest/ranges) with the hash of the exact tarball at payloadUrl,
so NO code path ever installs bytes no pin covered. Bundled versions/hashes refreshed 2026-07-23.
To add/bump an adapter: fetch the tarball, `sha256sum` it, update version+sha256+payloadUrl+probe
substring together, and re-run the in-VM install matrix. Never bump a version without its hash.
{payload} in installCmd expands to the staged, hash-verified file — the install MUST consume the
bytes the pin covered rather than re-resolving from a registry (that would defeat the pin).
apiKeyEnvVar names the env var the CLI reads its model API key from; the spawn path injects the
user's key under exactly that name (audit fix #13 — a hardcoded ANTHROPIC_API_KEY meant codex never
saw its key). Omit it for CLIs that authenticate interactively (opencode is multi-provider and
configures its own credentials in-terminal), and no key is injected at all.
engines note: the agent image now ships node 22 via Nix (/opt/toolchain) — the move that unblocked
gemini-cli (its bundle failed to parse on node 20: 'Invalid regular expression flags') and qwen-code
(engines node>=22). claude-code/codex/opencode were verified in the hardened jail on node 20 and
run on 22 (claude-code's own engines want >=22). @sourcegraph/amp is deliberately NOT here: it is a
timestamp-versioned platform-binary dispatcher (a new 'version' several times a day + a postinstall
that swaps the bin) — pinning it is meaningless and the updater would nag perpetually.
credentialPaths lists the $HOME-relative files where a CLI keeps its interactive-login state
(OAuth tokens + the account/onboarding records that make the login stick). The jail home is a
tmpfs wiped every relaunch, so Mainguard restores these at spawn from the HOST OS keychain and
harvests them back on stop — log in once, stay logged in. Files only, never directories (a
directory would drag session history/caches into the keychain). Keep the list to what the
vendor's CLI actually reads for auth; verify against the pinned version when bumping.
